phase: blockade + diplomacy (kinetic phase concluded per Rubio May 5)
epic_fury_status: concluded — pending MOU finalization; reversion possible. SecState Rubio (May 5 White House briefing): 'The Operation Epic Fury is concluded. We achieved the objectives of that operation.' Trump (May 6 Truth Social) conditioned conclusion on Iran's MOU acceptance, warning bombing would resume 'at a much higher level and intensity than it was before' otherwise.
dark_eagle_request_status: pending — CENTCOM Apr 29 request to deploy U.S. Army Long-Range Hypersonic Weapon (Dark Eagle) to AOR; weapon has not completed operational testing; not yet deployed
total_targets_struck: ~35,500+ (CENTCOM/JINSA: 2,000+ command-and-control strikes on Iran, 8,000+ Iranian military targets decimated, 155+ warships sunk (largest navy elimination since WWII), 80% air defenses destroyed, every submarine sunk, 97% naval mines eliminated, 70% of Iranian space-launch facilities degraded. Iran air force reduced from 30-100 daily sorties pre-war to ZERO today. Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~35,500+ through Day 40. CEASEFIRE on Iran front holds through Day 54 — NO confirmed US/Israeli strikes on Iranian territory since 8 PM ET Apr 7. APR 21 ~16:22 ET: Trump EXTENDED Iran ceasefire INDEFINITELY via Truth Social at request of Pakistan Field Marshal Munir and PM Sharif, pending an Iranian 'unified proposal'; directed military to maintain naval blockade and characterized Iran's government as 'seriously fractured.' Reversal came hours after Trump told CNBC's Squawk Box he did NOT expect to extend. US aircraft losses: 39 airframes across 13,000+ sorties. APR 19 'TOUSKA DAY' — FIRST MAJOR KINETIC BLOCKADE ENFORCEMENT: USS Spruance (DDG-111) intercepted Iranian-flagged M/V Touska in Gulf of Oman; after ~6-hour standoff, fired 5-inch MK45 rounds into engine room disabling propulsion; 31st MEU Marines rappelled from MH-60S Seahawks off USS Tripoli (LHA-7) for VBSS and took ship into custody. APR 22 HORMUZ ESCALATION: IRGC fired on three commercial vessels in Strait of Hormuz and seized two — Panama-flagged MSC Francesca and Greek-operated Epaminondas — escorting them to Iranian shores; third vessel Euphoria reportedly 'stranded on Iranian shores' per semi-official Iranian outlets (UNVERIFIED); Iran statement: 'Disrupting the order and safety of the Strait of Hormuz is our red line.' CENTCOM: 31 commercial vessels redirected since Apr 13 (Gen. Caine Apr 16: 13; Apr 18: 23; NPR Apr 21: 28; CENTCOM Apr 23: 31 vessels, 10,000+ soldiers, 17 warships, 100+ aircraft). APR 23 ROE ESCALATION: Trump ordered US Navy via Truth Social to 'shoot and kill any boat' laying mines in Strait of Hormuz — most significant rules-of-engagement change since Apr 13 blockade; Pentagon boarded another 'sanctioned stateless vessel' carrying Iranian oil in Indian Ocean overnight Apr 22-23; Reuters reports ≥3 Iranian-flagged tankers intercepted in Asian waters (redirected from near India, Malaysia, Sri Lanka). APR 23: NO new US/Israeli strikes on Iranian territory; ceasefire hold continues. Lebanon-Israel Round 2 direct ambassadors' talks convened in Washington (moved from State Dept to White House); Qatar proposed 20-for-20 Hormuz vessel swap compromise — reportedly blocked by Iran. US Senate defeated Baldwin war-powers resolution 55-46 (5th failed attempt). APR 17-18 HORMUZ WHIPSAW: FM Araghchi declared strait 'completely open' Apr 17 for duration of Lebanon ceasefire; crude plunged (WTI -11.45% to $82.59; Brent -~9% to $90.38); IRGC Navy REVERSED Apr 18. Kinetic incidents Apr 18: VLCC Sanmar Herald fired on by IRGC gunboats; CMA CGM Everglade rocket-damaged off Oman; cruise ship Mein Schiff 4 threatened via VHF. APR 21: FM Araghchi called Touska seizure and blockade 'an act of war and a material breach of the ceasefire'; UN Amb. Iravani filed formal protest to SecGen Guterres. ISLAMABAD ROUND 2 COLLAPSED Apr 22: Vance's trip cancelled after Iran declined to attend; Air Force Two had reached Joint Base Andrews before cancellation; Witkoff and Kushner remained in Washington; unresolved disputes — enriched uranium stockpile, Hormuz status, blockade, Iranian war-reparations demand. Apr 16 ISRAEL-LEBANON CEASEFIRE (effective 17:00 EST Apr 16) holds but fraying — Apr 21 IDF reported Hezbollah rocket fire at Rab Thalathin IDF position and drone into northern Israel; State Department ambassador-level Israel-Lebanon talks scheduled Apr 23-24 (Huckabee, Leiter, Moawad). 60-day War Powers deadline ~May 1.) APR 24 (DAY 56): Ceasefire on Iran continues to hold (no kinetic strikes on Iranian territory). CENTCOM publicly announced THREE U.S. carriers now operating simultaneously in the Middle East for the first time since Operation Iraqi Freedom (2003): USS Abraham Lincoln (CVN-72, Northern Arabian Sea), USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78, Red Sea), and USS George H.W. Bush (CVN-77, Indian Ocean) — combined >200 aircraft and 15,000 sailors/Marines. Ships redirected by U.S. blockade rose 31→34 (Navy Times Apr 24; SOF News cites 33). Iranian FM Araghchi flew to Islamabad evening of Apr 24 with small delegation; WH announced Special Envoy Witkoff and Jared Kushner depart Pakistan Apr 25 for direct US-Iran talks 'intermediated by the Pakistanis' (Iran's FM spokesman Baghaei publicly denies a US-Iran meeting). Pope Leo XIV aboard papal flight from Malabo: 'As a pastor, I cannot be in favor of war'; condemned Iranian regime killings of anti-government protesters. Israeli DefMin Katz (Apr 23) said Israel is 'awaiting a green light' to resume war and 'return Iran to the dark ages.' NYT (Apr 24) investigation: U.S. has expended >1,200 Patriot interceptors, ~1,100 long-range stealth cruise missiles (close to total stockpile), >1,000 Tomahawks (~10x annual procurement) during Epic Fury — WH Press Sec. Leavitt called premise 'false.' NYT/Fassihi (Apr 23): Mojtaba Khamenei sustained 'disfiguring' facial burns and remains in hiding. APR 27-30 (DAYS 59-62): Iran ceasefire holds — NO U.S./Israeli kinetic strikes on Iranian territory; pressure now exclusively maritime (blockade) and rhetorical. APR 28: Iran's revised peace proposal (Hormuz reopening + ceasefire extension in exchange for U.S. lifting blockade, with nuclear program DEFERRED) submitted via Pakistani mediators. APR 29: Trump explicitly REJECTS proposal in Axios phone interview ('they are choking like a stuffed pig'); meets with energy executives to plan blockade extension; posts 'NO MORE MR. NICE GUY!' image to Truth Social. CENTCOM (Adm. Brad Cooper): blockade has redirected 42 commercial vessels cumulative (up from 34). Pentagon Acting Comptroller Jules Hurst (HASC testimony Apr 29) pegged Operation Epic Fury direct cost at ~$25B (munitions/O&M/equipment-replacement only — CNN sourcing characterizes as lowball excluding base-repair, force-flow, intel costs). Hegseth: critics are 'biggest adversary,' 'reckless, feckless and defeatist.' APR 30: Brent crude HITS 4-YEAR HIGH $126.41/bbl intraday (Asia hours) before paring on Axios report that CENTCOM commander Adm. Cooper would brief President Trump on potential renewed strike options including reportedly 'a short and intense wave of strikes.' AAA national gas $4.30/gal (4-yr high). MAY 1 (DAY 63): War Powers Resolution 60-day statutory deadline arrives. H.Con.Res.38 (Massie/Khanna) at 94 cosponsors per congress.gov; latest Senate vote Apr 15 failed 52-47 (4th failure); Time scoop (Apr 28): Democrats may sue Trump if he ignores deadline.

aggregate_casualties_iran.killed: 2,000+ (Health Ministry) / 3,636 (HRANA) / 6,620 military (Hengaw) / 4,700+ security forces (Iran International) / 6,000+ IRGC (IDF claim)
aggregate_casualties_iran.sources: Iran Health Ministry (WHO Apr 10) / HRANA / Hengaw / Iran International / IDF / ACLED / AP / Christian Science Monitor

civilian_buildings_affected: ~20,000 (including 16,000 residential units) per Iranian Red Crescent as of Day 11
healthcare_facilities_affected: 307 health/medical/emergency facilities damaged per Iranian Red Crescent; 20+ verified healthcare attacks (WHO); 9 deaths from healthcare attacks
weapons_confirmed: B-2 Spirit bombers (37-hour CONUS round-trips from Whiteman AFB), Tomahawk cruise missiles (including Block Va Maritime Strike variant with radar-absorbent 'black' coating), GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrators, Precision Strike Missiles (first combat use), LUCAS low-cost attack drones (first combat use), 5,000-lb deep penetrator munitions
notes: Casualty figures are Iranian government claims via Red Crescent and health ministry; independently difficult to verify due to internet blackout. HRANA (US-based) figures broadly consistent. ACLED tracking ongoing. Scope of this dataset: strikes ON Iranian territory by US and Israeli forces only. Iranian counter-strikes and regional escalation tracked in separate dataset.

ceasefire_on_iran.status: INDEFINITE — Day 13 since Apr 21 extension, Day 26 since Apr 7 announcement; holding through May 4 (no kinetic strikes on Iranian sovereign territory Day 63-66) BUT CONTESTED/DEGRADING. RoE EXPANDED Day 65-66 (per Axios/Ravid): US forces in CENTCOM authorized to strike 'immediate threats' against ships transiting Hormuz (incl. IRGC fast boats, missile positions). Trump REJECTED CENTCOM Cdr Adm. Cooper's more aggressive 'forced entry' plan presented Apr 30; current posture is calibrated escalation. Day 66: PROJECT FREEDOM launched — US Navy began 'guiding' (not escorting) US-flagged commercial vessels through Strait of Hormuz; force package 15,000 troops, 100+ aircraft, 2 DDGs entered Persian Gulf. CENTCOM sank 6 IRGC small boats while facilitating passage of 2 US-flagged merchants; CENTCOM (@CENTCOM) DENIED Iranian Fars News claim that US warship was struck near Jask by 2 missiles ('No U.S. Navy ships have been struck'). Senior Iranian official to Reuters concedes only 'warning shot' fired. Day 66 Iranian retaliation pivoted to UAE: drone strike ignited fire at Fujairah Oil Industry Zone (VTTI/Habshan-Fujairah pipeline endpoint), 2x suicide drones hit empty ADNOC tanker 'Barakah' in Hormuz, UAE MoD intercepted 12 ballistic missiles + 3 cruise missiles + 4 UAVs (4th CM fell to sea); 3 Indian nationals moderately injured at Fujairah.
ceasefire_on_iran.expires_note: null — pending Iranian revised proposal (expected by Friday May 1 per CNN Apr 29)
ceasefire_on_iran.next_status_check: 2026-05-01 — War Powers 60-day deadline / UAE OPEC exit effective / Iran revised proposal expected
ceasefire_on_iran.day_59_note: APR 27: NO new US/Israeli strikes on Iranian territory; Iran ceasefire enters Day 19 since Apr 8 origin / Day 6 since Apr 21 indefinite extension. Via Pakistani mediators, Iran transmitted a written proposal to the U.S. offering to extend the ceasefire and reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for the U.S. lifting its naval blockade of Iranian ports — explicitly DEFERRING nuclear-program negotiations to a later phase (Axios primary; Bloomberg, AP, CNBC, Al Jazeera confirm). Trump told reporters Saturday: 'Interestingly, immediately, when I canceled it, within 10 minutes, we got a new paper that was much better,' but added Iran 'offered a lot but not enough.' WH Press Sec. Karoline Leavitt confirmed Trump convened a Situation Room meeting Apr 27 with national-security team to review the proposal; no public readout by EOD. WH spokesperson Olivia Wales: 'These are sensitive diplomatic discussions and the U.S. will not negotiate through the press... never allowing Iran to have a nuclear weapon.' SIMULTANEOUS IRANIAN COUNTER-SIGNAL: IRGC Telegram statement: 'Controlling the Strait of Hormuz and maintaining the shadow of its deterrent effects over America and the White House's supporters in the region is the definitive strategy of Islamic Iran.' Deputy Parliament Speaker Ali Nikzad (Mehr) cited a Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei order that Hormuz 'under no circumstances' returns to its prior state. The IRGC stance directly contradicts the simultaneously transmitted Iranian proposal — Axios reports Araghchi told Pakistani/Egyptian/Turkish/Qatari mediators 'there's no consensus inside the Iranian leadership.' RUSSIA TRACK ENTERS FRAME: Iran FM Araghchi arrived in St Petersburg early Monday to meet President Vladimir Putin at the Boris Yeltsin Presidential Library and FM Sergei Lavrov, capping multi-leg shuttle (Pakistan Apr 24, Oman Apr 26, Pakistan Apr 26, Russia Apr 27). Russia's Vienna envoy Mikhail Ulyanov said the U.S. must abandon 'blackmailing' and 'ultimatums.' EUROPEAN POSTURE: EC President Ursula von der Leyen said it is 'too early' to lift sanctions on Iran; German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said Iran is 'humiliating' the U.S. as peace talks stall. AP Apr 27: 'U.S. says it's clearing Iranian mines in latest push to open the Strait of Hormuz' — follow-up to Trump's 'shoot and kill any boat laying mines' order. Bloomberg shipping data: $500M Mordashov-linked superyacht NORD transited Hormuz Saturday — anomaly, one of very few vessels to clear dual blockade. Goldman Sachs raised Q4 2026 Brent forecast to $90/bbl (from $80) and WTI to $83/bbl (from $75) citing 14.5 mb/d Persian Gulf production losses driving 'record 11–12 mb/d' April global inventory draw. WAR POWERS: T-4 days to May 1 60-day mark; no Apr 27 floor action; last (5th) Senate WPR vote was Apr 22 (Baldwin) failed 46-51; Sen. Susan Collins indicated she may join Democrats post-deadline. APR 25-26: Trump CANCELLED Witkoff-Kushner Pakistan trip via Truth Social ('we have all the cards; they have none! If they want to talk, all they have to do is call'); reverted to phone-only diplomacy.
ceasefire_on_iran.day_60_61_note: APR 28-29 (Days 60-61): NO new US/Israeli strikes on Iranian territory; Iran ceasefire enters Day 21 since Apr 8 origin / Day 8 since Apr 21 indefinite extension. APR 28: 31st MEU Marines rappelled from helicopter to board Comoros-flagged container ship M/V Blue Star III in the Arabian Sea on suspicion of breaching the U.S. naval blockade — released after CENTCOM determined no Iranian port call was planned; CENTCOM said cumulative redirected vessels now total 39 (up from 34) and >20 ships now moored/anchored at Iran's Chah Bahar port (vs. ~5/day pre-blockade baseline). APR 28 (CBS News): Iranian state media (IRIB) revised the Feb 28 Minab Shajareh Tayyebeh elementary school strike toll DOWN to 155 killed (73 boys, 47 girls, 26 teachers, 7 parents, 1 bus driver, 1 pharmacy tech) from prior >175 figure. APR 28: Trump posts on Truth Social that Iran 'informed us that they are in a State of Collapse'; rebukes German Chancellor Friedrich Merz. APR 29: Trump tells Axios he has REJECTED Iran's Hormuz-for-blockade proposal — 'They want to settle. They don't want me to keep the blockade. I don't want to [lift it], because I don't want them to have a nuclear weapon... [the blockade is] somewhat more effective than the bombing... They are choking like a stuffed pig.' Trump says Iran's oil storage and pipelines 'are getting close to exploding.' APR 29: Trump-Putin phone call — Putin reportedly puts forward ideas on resolving Iran's nuclear program; Russia continues to be floated as possible recipient of Iran's HEU stockpile. APR 29: Iran FM Araghchi back in Tehran from Moscow; mediators in Pakistan tell CNN Iran's revised peace proposal is expected by Friday May 1; internal Iranian decision-making slowed by difficulty communicating with Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei (still in hiding since Feb 28). APR 29 (HASC testimony — first since war began): Acting Pentagon Comptroller Jules Hurst pegs cumulative Operation Epic Fury cost at ~$25 BILLION (mostly munitions); SecDef Hegseth says any Iran-specific supplemental request will be 'less than $25B'; Hegseth identifies 14 critical munitions for replenishment (Patriot, THAAD, SM-3, SM-6, AMRAAM, JASSM, PrSM among them). FY2027 Pentagon budget request: $1.5 trillion. APR 26: Iran banned export of all steel products effective Apr 26 (Fars/AFP via CBS) — civilian-economic ripple of war. APR 29: Iranian rial hits record low (AP). UAE confirms OPEC withdrawal effective May 1.
ceasefire_on_iran.day_57_58_note: APR 25-26: NO new US/Israeli strikes on Iranian territory. Bushehr NPP ~21 consecutive days without a recorded incident (last confirmed Apr 4-5 per IAEA reporting). APR 25 ~14:00 ET: Trump CANCELLED Witkoff-Kushner Pakistan trip via Truth Social ('we have all the cards; they have none! If they want to talk, all they have to do is call'); reverted to phone-only diplomacy. Iran FM Araghchi cycled Islamabad → Muscat (met Sultan Haitham) → back to Islamabad → bound for Moscow Apr 27 to meet Putin. Pezeshkian (IRIB) said Iran will not enter 'forced negotiations' under 'pressure and threats.' Tehran IKIA airport reopened to international flights APR 25 after 56-day shutdown (first departures: Istanbul, Muscat, Medina). U.S. blockade tally rose to 37 vessels (+M/V Sevan intercepted by USS Pinckney heli Apr 24-25). UK PM Starmer-Trump phone call APR 26 urged Hormuz reopening (first publicly confirmed UK-US Iran call of ceasefire phase). 60-day War Powers deadline May 1 — 5 days remaining; no AUMF, no formal extension certification submitted.

iran_nuclear_sites_surveillance: U.S. Space Force watching (per Trump Apr 21)
israel_posture: Defense Minister Israel Katz (Apr 23) said Israel is 'awaiting a green light' from the U.S. to resume war and 'complete the elimination of the Khamenei dynasty' / 'return Iran to the dark ages.' (CNN Apr 23)
supreme_leader_status: NYT (Farnaz Fassihi, Apr 23): Mojtaba Khamenei sustained 'disfiguring' facial burns, struggles to speak, remains in hiding. Internal IRGC commanders increasingly overriding civilian negotiators (Axios).
munitions_depletion_intel: NYT investigation Apr 24: U.S. expended ~1,200 Patriot interceptors (>$4M each), ~1,100 long-range stealth cruise missiles (close to entire remaining stockpile), >1,000 Tomahawks (≈10x annual procurement). AEI/McCusker total cost estimate $25B-$35B. WH Press Sec. Leavitt called premise 'false.' Source: Dawn/NYT https://www.dawn.com/news/1994691/us-has-exhausted-billions-of-dollars-worth-of-weapons-stockpile-in-war-on-iran-nyt-report
hormuz_strike_plan_contingency: CNN (Apr 23): U.S. planners preparing 'dynamic targeting' of small fast-attack boats, minelaying vessels, asymmetric assets, dual-use and infrastructure targets including energy facilities, and 'individual Iranian military leaders and other obstructionists' — should the ceasefire collapse. Source: https://www.cnn.com/2026/04/23/politics/us-military-plans-iran-targets-strait-of-hormuz

targets: IRGC Malek-Ashtar building (Mar 2, Day 3 — confirmed destroyed by Iran International footage)
IRIB state broadcaster HQ (early hours Mar 3, Day 4 — separate Israeli air operation)
notes: Continuous bombardment since Day 1. Supreme Leader Khamenei killed in initial strike on Leadership House compound along with his daughter, son-in-law, grandchild, and daughter-in-law Zahra Haddad-Adel. Defense Minister Aziz Nasirzadeh, IRGC commander Mohammad Pakpour, chief of staff Abdolrahim Mousavi, Ali Shamkhani, and multiple other senior officials also killed. Mojtaba Khamenei (son) injured but survived; named new Supreme Leader on Mar 8 (Day 9). Day 11 (Mar 10): 40+ killed in Resalat Square residential area strike in eastern Tehran (see tehran-east-residential). Day 12: IDF destroys IRGC Air Force HQ (see tehran-irgc-af-hq). 460+ killed and 4,300+ wounded in Tehran alone as of Day 11 per Tehran Emergency Health Department. Days 20-21: Ali Mohammad Naini (IRGC spokesperson) killed in IAF airstrike overnight Mar 19-20. Day 22: Continued strikes on Tehran military and government infrastructure. Day 24 (Mar 23): IDF launched 'broad wave of attacks' on Tehran. Day 25 (Mar 24): IAF struck 50+ targets overnight. Day 26 (Mar 25): IDF launches 'new wide-scale attacks' on Iranian government infrastructure, including Tehran targets. South Tehran strike kills at least 12, wounds 28. Eastern Tehran strikes destroy school and residential buildings. Day 33 (Apr 1): Airstrike hit former US Embassy compound on Taleghani Street — IRGC-controlled since 1979 — internally detonating penetrator. Russian embassy across the street confirmed blast broke doors/windows at St. Nicholas Orthodox Cathedral and partially collapsed adjacent Russian nursing home.

Primary nuclear targets struck in first wave. Deep penetrating munitions used. Day 22 (Mar 21): Natanz Shahid Ahmadi-Roshan Enrichment Complex struck with bunker-buster bombs (likely GBU-57 MOPs and/or GBU-72 5,000-lb penetrators). Israel denied involvement ('did not conduct any strikes in the area'). IAEA: 'No increase in off-site radiation levels.' DG Grossi called for 'military restraint to avoid any risk of nuclear accident.' Russia condemned as 'blatant violation of international law.' Entrance buildings of underground Fuel Enrichment Plant damaged. Bulk of Iran's enriched uranium (~440 kg) believed at Isfahan, not Natanz. Third strike on Natanz (after June 2025 Operation Midnight Hammer and early March 2026).

targets: Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant (perimeter and auxiliary buildings)
casualties_reported: Minimal — military targets. No radiation increase per IAEA.
notes: Struck 4 times during Epic Fury (Mar 17, 24, 27, Apr 4). IAEA confirmed no radiation increase. FM Araghchi warned Apr 4 that radioactive release would affect Gulf capitals — Bushehr closer to Kuwait City (175 mi) and Manama (187 mi) than Tehran. Russia's Rosatom evacuating staff (198 additional evacuated Apr 5). Plant holds 72 tons of fuel and 210 tons of spent fuel. IAEA has no inspector access to any Iranian nuclear facility; Iran's parliament suspended IAEA cooperation. Agency has 'lost continuity of knowledge' regarding nuclear material inventories. DAY 57 (Apr 25): 21 consecutive days no new incident; Russia (Rosatom) staff reportedly returning per Russian ambassador statement that Russia hopes work on Bushehr completion will resume soon.

Key Strait of Hormuz control point. Continuous strikes to suppress coastal batteries. Day 14: Continued strikes on remaining coastal defense positions as part of Strait of Hormuz suppression campaign. CENTCOM reported that within 48 hours of war start, all 11 Iranian warships in the Gulf of Oman had been denied presence. Three commercial ships struck by suspected Iranian projectiles in the strait on March 11. Day 23: Aerial campaign continues (Day 4) with A-10 Warthogs and Apache helicopters. Day 24 (Mar 23): One person killed and one wounded in Bandar Abbas from attack targeting Persian Gulf Broadcasting Center radio transmitter.

Iranian Air Force base. Runways cratered in first 90 minutes. IDF confirmed it has targeted 6 Iranian airbases total since Feb 28. CTP-ISW recorded strikes on 10 of Iran's 17 Artesh Air Force tactical airbases. Shiraz was struck again in the second week — see shiraz-missile-base for Day 8+ strikes.
Northwestern air base near Azerbaijan border.
Long-suspected nuclear weapons research site southeast of Tehran.
Plutonium production pathway. Struck to eliminate weapons-grade material route.
Deeply buried enrichment facility near Qom. GBU-57 bunker busters deployed.

90% of Iran's oil exports flow through Kharg. Struck in second-week escalation. Israel bombed 30 additional oil depots on Day 10. Day 15 (Mar 13): US executed large-scale precision strike on 90+ military targets including naval mine storage, missile storage bunkers, air defense facility, naval base, airport control tower/runway, and helicopter hangar. Oil infrastructure deliberately preserved. Trump warned of future strikes if Hormuz remains blocked. Trump called it 'one of the most powerful bombing raids in the History of the Middle East.' Day 16: Trump threatens to hit Kharg again 'just for fun.'
Major refinery. Strikes on oil infrastructure began March 7. Toxic smoke blanketed Tehran. Day 14: Continued strikes on remaining refinery capacity.

targets: IRGC Navy 'Sayyid al-Shuhada' military complex / Asif missile brigade HQ (intended target)
Shajareh Tayyebeh girls' elementary school (struck directly — adjacent to but walled off from IRGC base since 2016)
casualties_source: Iranian Red Crescent / IRNA / Iranian Ministry of Education; corroborated by NYT, CNN, NPR, BBC Verify, Bellingcat, TIME, Al Jazeera, OHCHR
notes: Deadliest confirmed civilian casualty incident of the war. Struck at approximately 10:45 AM local time on Day 1 during opening wave. School was triple-tapped per Iranian officials (mayor of Minab, Ministry of Education). Victims were primarily girls aged 7–12, plus teachers, parents, and the school principal. School had been physically separated from adjacent IRGC naval base by a wall and separate entrance since ~2016, with a visible soccer pitch in the courtyard by 2017 (confirmed by satellite imagery reviewed by HRW, NYT, CNN, TIME). Bellingcat and BBC Verify geolocated footage of a US Tomahawk cruise missile striking the area. Eight munitions experts confirmed to the Washington Post the missile was a Tomahawk. Pentagon preliminary internal investigation found US was likely responsible due to outdated DIA intelligence that failed to distinguish the school from the military compound. CNN reported CENTCOM created target coordinates using outdated DIA information. Trump initially blamed Iran; munitions experts confirmed no Iranian missile matches the weapon in verified footage. US is the only belligerent operating Tomahawk missiles. Under formal Pentagon investigation expected to take months.
Western Iran IRGC hub. Key missile launch suppression target.
Home to Shahab and Fattah ballistic missile units.
Southeastern naval base. Ghadir-class submarine facilities targeted.
Northeastern Iran. Iran's second-largest city.
Israeli military announced destruction of IRGC Air Force HQ on Day 12. Major command node eliminated.

targets: Residential apartment buildings near Resalat Square, Narmak neighborhood
Iranian police building on same street
casualties_source: Tasnim News Agency / Iranian Red Crescent (spokesman Mojtaba Khaledi) / Drop Site News / Al Jazeera
notes: Strike hit at 1:00 AM on March 10 (during Ramadan, when shops were open late and people were gathering). Three residential buildings bombed simultaneously on the same street, plus a police building. One of the deadliest attacks on Tehran since the start of the war. Rescue crews searched rubble for survivors. Second major civilian casualty incident in Tehran after Niloofar Square (Day 3, 20 killed).

targets: IRGC Aerospace Force Ali Akbar Drone Base (runway cratered — confirmed by satellite imagery Mar 8)
casualties_reported: Unknown — military targets; hospital collateral damage reported separately
casualties_source: Critical Threats / ISW / IDF / satellite imagery
notes: Multiple military targets struck across Ahvaz over the course of the war. Aboozar Children's Hospital in Ahvaz reported damaged (not directly struck) as early as March 2 per Iranian authorities and Al Jazeera. IDF announced March 13 strikes on 'headquarters of various regime bodies' in Ahvaz simultaneously with Tehran and Shiraz. Iranian Deputy Health Minister Ali Jafarian reported 31 major clinical facilities and hospitals damaged nationwide, 12 rendered inactive, as of Day 14. WHO verified 13 attacks on healthcare infrastructure in Iran.

targets: Military and government sites (Day 1 target city)
Continued strikes on Karaj, Fardis, Malard, Garmdareh suburbs in subsequent days
casualties_source: Wikipedia (2026 Israeli–United States strikes on Iran) / NCRI local accounts
notes: Listed alongside Tehran, Isfahan, Qom, and Kermanshah as a Day 1 target city. Western Tehran suburb with significant military infrastructure. Local accounts on Day 13 described ~20 explosions in Shahriar, ~30 in Malard/Fardis, 7-8 in Garmdareh.

casualties_source: Wikipedia (2026 Israeli–United States strikes on Iran)
notes: Listed as a Day 1 target city in multiple sources alongside Tehran, Isfahan, Qom, Karaj, and Kermanshah.

targets: Military and government sites in Qom city proper (distinct from Fordow enrichment facility)
casualties_source: Multiple sources list Qom as Day 1 target city / NCRI reports 6 explosions on Day 13
notes: Qom city listed as a Day 1 target alongside Tehran, Isfahan, Karaj, Kermanshah, and Kerman. Fordow enrichment facility (near Qom) tracked separately. Continued strikes reported in subsequent days.

Southern coastal naval base. Confirmed struck by satellite imagery published by USNI News (March 1 image). Located in Sistan-Baluchistan province, 400km southeast of Minab. Part of US southern flank naval suppression campaign (Operation Epic Fury).

targets: Structures flanked by earthen berms (weapons/munitions research)
casualties_source: Critical Threats / ISW / satellite imagery (March 7 capture)
notes: Military complex east of Tehran. Satellite imagery captured March 7 confirmed several structures destroyed. Long associated with weapons research programs.

targets: Golestan Palace complex (UNESCO World Heritage Site — damaged by strike on Arg Square, Mar 2)
Azadi Stadium (Iran's largest sports complex — bombed, Day 6+)
Oil storage/fuel depots near Tehran (Israeli strikes Mar 7–8, caused 'river of fire' and toxic black smoke/acid rain over city)
Gandhi Hospital (damaged by nearby strike on state TV tower, Mar 2 — IVF department destroyed)
Schools in Parand, southwest Tehran (two schools struck)
Narmak neighborhood school (damaged Day 3, at least 2 children killed)
casualties_reported: Multiple incidents — see individual notes; 460+ killed in Tehran alone as of Day 11
casualties_source: Iranian MFA / Red Crescent / WHO (verified 13 attacks on healthcare in Iran) / Al Jazeera / AP / NCRI
notes: Aggregated entry for confirmed civilian infrastructure damage across Tehran beyond the dedicated residential and military entries. Iranian MFA claimed 33 civilian locations struck nationwide including hospitals, schools, residential areas, the Grand Bazaar, Golestan Palace, and Azadi Stadium. Iranian Red Crescent reported 3,600+ civilian sites damaged as of Day 6; 20,000 civilian buildings including 16,000 residential units affected as of Day 11. WHO verified 13 attacks on healthcare infrastructure in Iran (4 healthcare workers killed, 25 injured, 4 ambulances affected). 77 healthcare facilities affected per Red Crescent; 31 major clinical facilities/hospitals damaged and 12 rendered inactive per Deputy Health Minister. Niloofar Square strike (Day 3) killed 20 civilians per state media.

targets: Artesh Air Force 8th Tactical Airbase (F-14 jets destroyed, confirmed by IDF Mar 7)
casualties_source: Critical Threats / ISW / IDF / satellite imagery (March 8 capture)
notes: Distinct from Isfahan nuclear facilities (tracked under isfahan-nuclear). Ground-penetrating munitions used on underground missile silos. IDF confirmed on March 8 that IAF strikes on March 7 destroyed F-14 fighter jets at an airbase in Isfahan, likely the 8th Tactical Airbase.

targets: Underground ballistic missile production and storage facility south of Shiraz
Shiraz Air Base (additional strikes beyond Day 1-2)
casualties_source: Critical Threats / ISW / IDF / satellite imagery (March 7 capture)
notes: Separate from the shiraz-airbase entry which covers Day 1-2 strikes. Satellite imagery March 7 showed ground-penetrating munitions used on underground facilities at a missile base south of Shiraz — one of at least 25 Iranian medium-range ballistic missile bases. June 2025 strikes had caused only light above-ground damage. IDF announced on March 13 that underground missile production site in Shiraz was struck. NCRI reports heavy strikes on Shiraz from early March 13, including airport, control tower, Gulf Highway, Moharrab Street, and SaIran complex.

targets: LEC targets in Kuhdasht, Eslamabad-e Gharb, Abdanan
notes: Part of the combined force campaign against Iran's internal security apparatus. LEC institutions targeted in cities that saw significant anti-government protests in Dec 2025–Jan 2026. CTP-ISW confirmed protests had occurred in Eslamabad-e Gharb, Borujerd, and Abdanan during the protest wave.

targets: South Pars natural gas field — Phases 3, 4, 5, 6
notes: CRITICAL ESCALATION — First strike on Iran's upstream energy infrastructure since Operation Epic Fury began. Israeli Air Force with explicit US coordination struck South Pars Phases 3/4/5/6 and Asaluyeh hub early Wednesday March 18. Two refineries taken offline with combined capacity of ~100 million cubic meters/day of gas — roughly one-fifth of the field's total capacity. South Pars produces 730 million cm³ of gas/day, supplying 70% of Iran's domestic gas. Qatar immediately condemned the strike. Trump claimed US 'knew nothing' (sources contradicted). Netanyahu confirmed Mar 19 Israel would comply with Trump's demand to stop further energy strikes. IAEA DG Grossi told NPR enrichment capabilities will partially survive, contradicting Netanyahu. IRGC issued 'urgent warning' naming SAMREF, Jubail, Al Hosn, Mesaieed, Ras Laffan as retaliatory targets. Brent spiked to $119.11 intraday; TTF gas +30-33%.

targets: Hardened Iranian anti-ship cruise missile sites along Hormuz coastline
notes: CENTCOM announced via social media that US forces employed multiple 5,000-pound deep penetrator munitions against hardened Iranian anti-ship cruise missile sites along the Strait of Hormuz coastline. CENTCOM stated the missiles 'posed a risk to international shipping in the strait.' No formal CENTCOM press releases published March 17-18; last official release dates to March 14.

casualties_source: Iranian state media / CNN live updates
notes: Iranian state media reported early Wednesday strikes on northern Tehran neighborhood. Emergency crews recovered two bodies. Part of continued bombardment of Tehran that has killed 460+ in the capital since Day 1.

targets: Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant — projectile strike
notes: Projectile struck Bushehr nuclear power plant on March 18. No injuries reported per IAEA and Rosatom. ~480 Russian citizens remain on site. FLAG: single-source on specifics. Separate from earlier Day 1-2 perimeter strikes tracked under bushehr-nuclear entry.

targets: Ali Larijani — Secretary of Supreme National Security Council
casualties_reported: Ali Larijani killed — confirmed by Iranian state media
casualties_source: Al Jazeera / Times of Israel / CNN / CBS
notes: Third most senior Iranian official killed since the war began. Funeral procession drew thousands March 18.

targets: Gholamreza Soleimani — Basij paramilitary organization chief
casualties_reported: Gholamreza Soleimani killed — confirmed by Iranian state media
notes: Fourth most senior Iranian official killed since the war began. Killed same day as Larijani.

casualties_reported: Esmail Khatib killed — confirmed by Iranian state media
casualties_source: NPR / Al Jazeera / Times of Israel
notes: Fifth most senior Iranian official killed since the war began. Third killed in 24 hours (after Larijani and Soleimani on March 17). Systematic decapitation of Iranian leadership continues without producing capitulation.

targets: Iranian Navy Northern Fleet vessels at Bandar Anzali
Naval infrastructure on Caspian Sea coast
notes: MAJOR GEOGRAPHIC ESCALATION — Israel's first strikes on Iranian naval targets in the Caspian Sea, ~1,300km from Israel and bordering Russia. More than 5 vessels targeted per Axios. Bandar Anzali hosts Iran's Northern Fleet HQ and is a key node in the Iran-Russia weapons supply corridor.

targets: Ali Mohammad Naini — IRGC spokesperson and head of public relations
notes: IRGC spokesperson killed in IAF airstrike overnight March 19-20. Part of ongoing systematic elimination of senior Iranian officials.

targets: General Afshin Nakshabandi — representative of Armed Forces HQ in Basij
notes: Representative of Iran's Armed Forces Headquarters in the Basij organization. Killed March 19-20.

targets: Esmail Ahmadi — IRGC head of intelligence
notes: IRGC head of intelligence. Killed March 19-20. Part of systematic decapitation campaign that has now eliminated 9+ senior Iranian officials.

targets: Mehdi Karishi — Commander of IRGC Aerospace Forces, Isfahan sector
notes: Commander of IRGC Aerospace Forces, Isfahan sector. Killed March 19-20. Ballistic missile launch rate down ~90% from Day 1.
Strikes expanded to Yazd as part of Day 20-21 record-tempo operations. Hegseth: 'largest strike package yet.'
Bandar Lengeh coastal area struck as part of continued Strait of Hormuz suppression campaign. Part of Day 20-21 record-tempo operations.

targets: Hardened underground facilities along southern coastline — anti-ship cruise missiles, mobile launchers, maritime surveillance equipment
Underground coastal facility storing anti-ship cruise missiles and mobile launchers (Mar 22)
Intelligence support sites and missile radar relays (Mar 22)
notes: Day 22-23: US employed GBU-72 5,000-pound bunker-buster bombs against hardened underground facilities along Iran's southern coastline. Day 22: Anti-ship cruise missiles, mobile launchers, and maritime surveillance equipment struck. Day 23 (Mar 22): Adm. Cooper video update confirmed destruction of underground coastal facility storing anti-ship cruise missiles and mobile launchers, plus 'intelligence support sites and missile radar relays.' Iran's combat capability 'on steady decline, as our offensive strikes ramp up.' Part of Day 4 of aerial campaign (begun Mar 19) using A-10 Warthogs and Apache gunships.

targets: Iranian military infrastructure — precision strike missiles
notes: US Army executed what CENTCOM called the 'longest field artillery strike in Army combat history' on March 19, using precision strike missiles against Iranian military infrastructure. First operational combat use of this capability at extreme range.
Day 24 (Mar 23): Iranian Red Crescent responded to strikes on a residential area in Urmia, northwest Iran. First reported strike on Urmia — extends campaign to West Azerbaijan province.
Day 24 (Mar 23): Fars news agency reported strikes in a residential area of Khorramabad killed and wounded several people. Khorramabad is the capital of Lorestan province in western Iran.

targets: HQ of Iran-backed group — senior commander Saad Dawai targeted
casualties_reported: Unknown — senior commander Saad Dawai targeted
notes: Day 25 (Mar 24): US strike in Iraq's Anbar Province targeted the headquarters of an Iran-backed group. Senior commander Saad Dawai specifically targeted. Part of ongoing US campaign against Iranian proxy forces in Iraq.

Day 25 (Mar 24): Strike destroyed IRIB (Islamic Republic of Iran Broadcasting) transmitter in Bandar Abbas, killing 1 person. 100kW AM radio station. Part of ongoing campaign to degrade Iranian state communications infrastructure.

targets: Multiple impact sites in Qazvin (northwestern Iran)
notes: Day 26 (Mar 25): Airstrikes hit Qazvin in northwestern Iran overnight. Witnesses reported multiple impacts. Part of IDF 'new wide-scale attacks' on Iranian government infrastructure.
Day 26 (Mar 25): IDF struck Iranian submarine research center in Isfahan as part of wide-scale attacks on Iranian government and military infrastructure.

targets: South Tehran targets — at least 12 killed, 28 wounded
Eastern Tehran — school and residential buildings destroyed
casualties_reported: At least 12 killed, 28 wounded in south Tehran; eastern Tehran school and residential buildings destroyed
notes: Day 26 (Mar 25): South Tehran strike killed at least 12, wounded 28. Eastern Tehran strikes destroyed school and residential buildings. Part of IDF 'new wide-scale attacks' on Iranian government infrastructure.

targets: Covert IRGC naval command center — IRGC Navy Commander Rear Admiral Alireza Tangsiri killed
Senior IRGC naval operational staff including intelligence and operations chiefs
casualties_reported: IRGC Navy Commander Tangsiri killed along with senior operational staff
notes: Day 27 (Mar 26): Israel's precision strike on covert naval command center in Bandar Abbas killed IRGC Navy Commander Rear Admiral Alireza Tangsiri — the architect of the Strait of Hormuz blockade. Based on joint US-Israeli intelligence. Also killed senior intelligence and operations chiefs. Defense Minister Katz called it 'significant news for American partners' and a direct step toward reopening the Strait. Tangsiri had personally ordered Hormuz closure on March 2.

targets: Remaining missile and drone production infrastructure in central Isfahan
casualties_reported: Unknown — strikes 'increasing in number and in intensity' per Al Jazeera
notes: Day 27 (Mar 26): Israel launched 'wave of extensive strikes' on central Isfahan targeting remaining missile and drone production infrastructure. Israeli military sources told NPR they want 'several more weeks of war' and are 'speeding up targeting over the next 48 hours' focusing on arms factories. US has now struck two-thirds of Iran's missile and drone production facilities. Day 28 (Mar 27): Continued strikes on weapons production facilities in Isfahan.

targets: Iran's 'most central' naval arms production facility
casualties_reported: Iranian soldiers killed at sites per IDF
notes: Day 28 (Mar 27): IDF conducted 'wide-scale wave of strikes' in 'the heart of Tehran' overnight, hitting dozens of weapon manufacturing sites. IDF Spokesman Brig. Gen. Effie Defrin confirmed Iranian soldiers killed. IDF has now struck more than 1,000 targets in Iran's weapons production sector alone.
Day 28 (Mar 27): IAF dropped approximately 70 bombs on missile launch sites, ballistic missile storage, and air defense systems in Kermanshah and Dezful in western Iran overnight.

targets: Military/government targets — 6 civilians killed from overnight strikes
notes: Day 28 (Mar 27): Six civilians reported killed in Qom from overnight strikes. Additional casualties reported in Urmia from strikes on residential buildings.
Day 30 (Mar 29): Israel struck the Shahid Khondab Heavy Water Complex in Arak. IAEA confirmed no radiological release. Part of continued targeting of Iran's nuclear infrastructure.
Day 30 (Mar 29): Israel struck the Ardakan yellowcake production plant in Yazd Province. IAEA confirmed no radiological release. Targets uranium mining and conversion capacity.

targets: Iran University of Science and Technology — severely damaged
casualties_reported: Unknown — IRGC issued ultimatum threatening retaliatory attacks on US/Israeli university campuses in Gulf
notes: Day 30 (Mar 29): US-Israeli strikes severely damaged the Iran University of Science and Technology and completed 'extensive' waves against dozens of Tehran infrastructure targets. IRGC responded with ultimatum demanding US condemn strikes on Iranian universities by 12:00 noon March 30, threatening retaliatory attacks on Texas A&M and Northwestern in Qatar, NYU in UAE.
Day 30 (Mar 29): A 10,000-cubic-meter water reservoir in Haftkel, Khuzestan Province was destroyed. Prompted critical IRGC ultimatum threatening attacks on US/Israeli university campuses in the Gulf.
Day 30 (Mar 29): Steel and cement factories across Isfahan and southern Iran struck as part of expanded infrastructure targeting campaign.
Day 29-30 (Mar 28-29): Bandar Khamir port city struck by joint US-Israeli airstrikes. At least 5 killed.
Day 28 (Mar 27): Steel plants in Isfahan struck as part of expanded industrial targeting campaign.
Day 28 (Mar 27): Steel plants in Ahvaz struck as part of expanded industrial targeting campaign.

Day 29 (Mar 28): US-Israeli airstrikes hit the yellowcake facility in Yazd Province. Part of expanded nuclear infrastructure targeting campaign. IAEA warned of potential radiological risks from strikes on nuclear facilities.

Day 29 (Mar 28): US-Israeli airstrikes hit the Khondab Heavy Water Complex in Arak. Prompted IAEA warnings about striking nuclear facilities. Multiple Iranian parliamentarians pushing NPT withdrawal legislation in response.

targets: Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant perimeter (3 projectiles)
casualties_reported: Unknown — IAEA warning of potential major radiological incident
notes: Day 32 (Mar 31): At least three projectiles landed near Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant, prompting IAEA warnings of a potential major radiological incident. ~480 Russians on site at the reactor facility.

targets: Tofigh Daru pharmaceutical factory — alleged fentanyl precursor/chemical weapons facility
casualties_reported: R&D department destroyed; 10 nurses killed 'at the scene of their service'
notes: Day 33 (Apr 1): Israel claimed Tofigh Daru supplied fentanyl precursors for alleged chemical weapons program. Iran acknowledged strike destroyed R&D department, calling it 'a blow to the national medical supply chain.' 10 nurses killed.

targets: Multiple military targets in Kerman and Sirjan, southern Iran
notes: Day 33 (Apr 1): US-Israeli strikes hit multiple targets in Kerman and Sirjan, southern Iran. Iranian media confirmed explosions.

targets: Eastern and northwestern sector targets in Ahvaz and Mahshahr, Khuzestan
casualties_reported: Unknown — explosions reported in eastern and northwestern sectors
notes: Day 33 (Apr 1): Explosions in eastern Ahvaz and northwestern Mahshahr from ongoing US-Israeli strikes in Khuzestan Province.
Day 33 (Apr 1): Hormozgan Province official confirmed 'enemy fighter jets' bombed the civilian passenger pier. No casualties reported.

casualties_reported: No casualties — freshwater supply threatened for island population
notes: Day 33 (Apr 1): Desalination plant knocked offline, threatening freshwater supply to Qeshm Island's population near the Strait of Hormuz.
Day 34 (Apr 2): US-Israeli forces struck a century-old medical research center in Tehran. Part of 170 IDF targets in prior 24 hours.
Day 34 (Apr 2): Bridge near Tehran, steel plants, and pharmaceutical facilities struck as part of intensified post-speech campaign.
Day 34 (Apr 2): Passenger pier in Bandar Abbas struck.
Day 34 (Apr 2): Desalination plant on Qeshm Island in the Strait of Hormuz struck — threatens freshwater supply to island population.
Day 34 (Apr 2): CNN geolocated video of large explosions near Baharestan, approximately 13 miles south of Isfahan.

targets: Former FM Kamal Kharazi — seriously injured, wife killed
notes: Day 33-34: Former Iranian FM Kamal Kharazi was seriously injured and his wife killed in an airstrike on April 1. Iran described this as a deliberate attempt to derail diplomacy — Kharazi had been overseeing the Pakistan back-channel for a possible meeting between VP Vance and Iranian officials.

targets: Strike mission over Iran — F-15E Strike Eagle shot down by Iranian air defenses
casualties_reported: Both crew recovered. Pilot rescued Day 36. WSO (Colonel, 494th Fighter Squadron, RAF Lakenheath) rescued Apr 5 after 48+ hours evading IRGC in Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad province. A-10 Warthog also downed during CSAR (pilot nursed aircraft to Kuwait before ejecting). Three rescue aircraft took fire. No US casualties during extraction.
notes: Day 36-37: F-15E Strike Eagle shot down over Iran Apr 3 — first US combat aircraft loss to enemy fire since 2003. WSO rescued Apr 5 after 48+ hours evading capture. Operation involved dozens of aircraft, a temporary remote airbase established inside Iran, and a CIA deception campaign that fed IRGC false intelligence. Two C-130 Hercules became stuck at improvised airstrip and were intentionally destroyed; three replacement C-130s flown in. A-10 also lost near Strait of Hormuz during CSAR — pilot nursed aircraft to Kuwaiti airspace before ejecting. Total confirmed US aircraft losses: 7 airframes + 12+ MQ-9 drones. Trump confirmed WSO recovered 'safe and sound.'

targets: EA-37B Compass Call II electronic warfare aircraft — first-ever operational deployment
notes: Day 35-36 (Apr 2-3): EA-37B Compass Call II electronic warfare aircraft deployed to CENTCOM theater — first-ever operational use of the platform. Marks significant expansion of US electronic warfare capabilities against Iranian air defenses and communications networks.
Day 36 (Apr 3): Shahid Beheshti University struck — part of ongoing campaign hitting 30+ Iranian universities. Strikes on academic institutions have drawn international condemnation.

targets: Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant auxiliary building
casualties_reported: No radiation increase. Russia evacuated additional 198 staff from Bushehr.
notes: Day 37 (Apr 4): Explosions hit Bushehr nuclear plant auxiliary building — 4th strike on Bushehr during Epic Fury (Mar 17, 24, 27, Apr 4). IAEA confirmed no radiation increase. FM Araghchi warned radioactive release would affect Gulf capitals — Bushehr closer to Kuwait City (175 mi) and Manama (187 mi) than to Tehran. Russia's Rosatom evacuated additional 198 staff. IAEA has no inspector access to any Iranian nuclear facility.
Day 37 (Apr 4-5): Explosions hit the Mahshahr Special Petrochemical Zone in Khuzestan province. At least 5 killed and 170+ injured. Major industrial target in Iran's oil-rich southwestern province.

targets: Combat Search and Rescue — WSO recovery operation with makeshift airbase inside Iran
casualties_reported: No American casualties. 2 C-130s intentionally destroyed. 3 replacement C-130s flown in. 3 rescue aircraft took fire at low altitude.
notes: Day 37 (Apr 5): WSO (Colonel, 494th FS, RAF Lakenheath) recovered after 48+ hours evading IRGC in mountains of southwestern Iran. Operation involved dozens of aircraft, temporary remote airbase constructed inside Iran, and CIA deception campaign feeding Iranian forces false intelligence. Two C-130 Hercules became stuck at improvised airstrip and intentionally destroyed to prevent capture; three replacement C-130s flown in. Iran claimed two C-130s and two Black Hawks shot down — US acknowledged C-130 destructions were deliberate. Demonstrated extraordinary operational reach. Trump confirmed WSO recovered 'safe and sound.'

targets: 50+ military targets on Kharg Island — Iran's primary oil export hub handling 90% of oil exports
casualties_reported: Unknown — military targets, not oil facilities directly
notes: Day 39 (Apr 7): US struck 50+ military targets on Kharg Island in early hours. Kharg handles approximately 90% of Iran's oil exports. US official told NBC strikes targeted military infrastructure, not oil facilities directly — mirroring calibrated approach of March 13 raid. Represents most significant escalation on Iran's economic chokepoint.

casualties_reported: 2 killed, 3 injured at Kashan railway bridge
notes: Day 39 (Apr 7): IDF launched 'wide-scale wave of strikes targeting dozens of infrastructure sites' across Iran. IDF issued 'urgent warning' in Farsi telling all Iranians to stay away from trains and railway lines for 12 hours. Appears to be opening phase of Trump's threatened 'Bridge Day' campaign, executed by Israel ahead of 8 PM ET deadline.

Day 39 (Apr 6-7): At least 18 civilians including 2 children killed in US-Israeli attack on Alborz Province. Tehran residential areas also struck with Red Crescent rescue workers responding to collapsed buildings.

casualties_source: Iranian state media / Iran's Jewish parliamentary representative
notes: Day 39 (Apr 7): Tehran synagogue (Rafi-Nia/Khorasaniha) 'completely destroyed' by a projectile. Condemned by Iran's Jewish parliamentary representative. Raises questions about targeting protocols.

targets: Sharif University of Technology — national AI platform data center and fuel station
notes: Day 39 (Apr 7): Sharif University of Technology campus hit, damaging Iran's national AI platform data center and a fuel station.

targets: Iran's largest petrochemical complex — South Pars (~50% of petrochemical production)
notes: Day 39 (Apr 7): Israel struck Iran's largest petrochemical complex in Asaluyeh (South Pars), responsible for approximately 50% of petrochemical production.

targets: Shahid Rezayee Nejad yellowcake production facility
notes: Day 39 (Apr 7): Strike on yellowcake production facility (Shahid Rezayee Nejad) in Ardakan, Yazd Province. Iran's Atomic Energy Organization condemned as a violation of peaceful nuclear facility protections.
Day 39 (Apr 7): Strikes on three Tehran airports (Bahram, Mehrabad, Azmayesh) targeting Iranian air force aircraft and helicopters.

Day 39 (Apr 7, post-ceasefire): Israel retaliated against post-ceasefire Iranian missile violations by attacking a radar installation near Tehran. This was the last confirmed strike before the ceasefire stabilized. Two-week ceasefire in effect from 8 PM ET Apr 7.

platform: F/A-18E Super Hornet (USS Abraham Lincoln, CVN-72)
targets: M/T Hasna (Iranian-flagged 333m oil tanker) — rudder disabled by F/A-18E gun engagement May 6
outcome: Rudder disabled; vessel unable to complete transit to Iran
casualties_reported: None reported (Iranian crew warned 6 hours before strike per CENTCOM)
casualties_source: CENTCOM official statement May 6 09:00 ET / 14:00 UTC
verification: CENTCOM official statement May 6 09:00 ET / 14:00 UTC
notes: First publicly known case of an aircraft directly disabling a vessel under the US naval blockade (compare USS Spruance/MV Touska Apr 19 destroyer-gun engagement). Demonstrates blockade tightening even as Project Freedom escorts paused.
